sim_hash_policy_note: Re-baselined 2026-07-23 for FID-0046 opaque-pool pointer canonicalization. Widescreen widen ON/OFF remains byte-identical; fire-rate ON/OFF remains distinct.
note: 30s scripted forward+strafe walk on the Dam under the determinism envelope (GE007_AUTO_FORWARD/RIGHT baked into the captured OSContPad stream). Byte-exact replay: record==replay1==replay2. Regenerate the tape only via tools/fidelity/tape_regression.sh --record; the hash is the regression guard. Re-baselined 2026-07-09 (17d978581466799f→95944e2282a48178): FID-0030 hash-region expansion (stan/collision scratch, g_BgRoomInfo, g_randomSeed) changed the hash COMPUTATION; sim behavior byte-identical (trace/screenshot unchanged, sim_invariance OFF==ON). Not a regression. Re-baselined 2026-07-10 (95944e2282a48178→11f0b8efb41593a3): FID-0066 (P1a/D2) — guard chr=6 (KF7 rate=3) sustains full-auto in this route; the default (Input.FireRateAuthentic ON) now fires the guard's gate 1/3 as often (N64-faithful cadence, symmetric with the player's FID-0056 fix). SAME input tape; only the guard-fire sim stream slows. The pre-FID-0066 hash 95944e2282a48178 is exactly reproduced under the opt-out GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0 (asserted by tools/fidelity/guard_fire_rate_symmetry_smoke.sh). Faithful sim change, not a regression. Re-baselined 2026-07-11 (11f0b8efb41593a3->580b481aa253ec17) for FID-0014: the faithful WAYMODE_MAGIC patrol semantics (default ON) freeze unseen patrolling/gopos guards and pad-warp them per retail chrTickBeams US 0x7F020EF0 / chrlvTravelTickMagic US 0x7F028600, so offscreen patroller positions (sim state) differ from the legacy creep-walk. GE007_NO_PATROL_MAGIC_FIX=1 reproduces the previous hash 11f0b8efb41593a3 byte-identically (verified on this tape). See docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0014-patrol-magic.md. Re-baselined 2026-07-12 (580b481aa253ec17->d843515404e1b652) for FID-0046: the effect-buffer zero-init (default ON, initexplosioncasing.c) zeroes g_ExplosionBuffer free-slot dead words (parts[].pos/size/rot, read only when frame>0) that this level's file-load left non-zero in the hashed pool region. Gameplay is byte-identical -- the words are dead memory the game never reads; screenshots are identical fix-ON vs fix-OFF. GE007_NO_EFFECT_BUF_ZERO_INIT=1 reproduces the prior hash 580b481aa253ec17 byte-identically (opt-out A/B verified across 3 runs). See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0046.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-12 (d843515404e1b652->33f29cf9d50267c2) for FID-0117: the root-motion seed flag in modelSetAnimFrame2WithChrStuff now reads rwdata->unk01 (offset 1, retail) instead of the port's erroneous rwdata->unk02 (offset 2). The flag is written to unk01 by modelSetAnimation2 and read at offset 1 by retail (ASM 7F0702EC: lb 1(v0), stored to sp+0x94==save_unk01); the port read the wrong adjacent byte, altering character root-motion accumulation on animation transitions. GE007_NO_ANIMFRAME2_ROOTFLAG_FIX=1 reproduces the prior hash d843515404e1b652 byte-identically (A/B verified across all 7 tapes). Faithful sim change, not a regression. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0117.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-15 (33f29cf9d50267c2->8ce67f5a6df69ef0) for FID-0131: build-environment (toolchain/link-layout) sensitivity of the Dam sim-state hash. The hashed 8 MB pool region includes build-layout-sensitive dead/uninitialized bytes (the FID-0046 class) that the Dam's transient file-load scratch leaves non-zero in bump-allocated stage buffers the game never reads. Proven dead: a binary built at the prior toolchain (33f29cf9) and the current build (8ce67f5a) replay THIS tape with a BYTE-IDENTICAL 1800-frame --trace-state -- only 1133 pool bytes across 14 free-slot buffer blocks differ, all 26 non-pool hash regions identical -- so gameplay is unchanged and only the dead-memory fingerprint moved. A durable zero-init/exclusion fix is BLOCKED (the dead bytes span ~14 generic stage buffers, not a single Dam-only buffer, so any allocation-site zero-init would also change non-Dam hashes = too broad). Not a regression. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0131.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-19 (8ce67f5a6df69ef0->c6d1bd05d67a8902) for FID-0132: the collision-hull builder sub_GAME_7F03ECC0 now stores the retail (unclamped) hull vertex count on the collision_data-backed object/door path (rect==&data->unk04, spare words unk24..unk40 are the retail overflow slots); the FID-0096 clamp-to-4 remains only on the bare-rect4f tank path. The old unconditional clamp dropped real hull corners for thin/near-axis-aligned box footprints (degenerate collision polygons -> broken point-in-polygon -> object grounding errors, e.g. the Dam control-room monitors floor-snapping 93 units below their desks; 116 spurious-degenerate footprints across dam/facility/archives/streets -> 0). Corrected footprints change object collision/grounding sim state on every object-bearing level. GE007_NO_HULL_CORNER_FIX=1 reproduces the prior hash 8ce67f5a6df69ef0 byte-identically (verified on this tape on the fixed binary). Faithful sim change, not a regression. Corpus ctest hull_builder guards the geometry. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0132.json. fire_rate_optout variant likewise re-based 5d8b8a0ecd40de24->aff5de8ca6522bc5 (= fix-ON default + GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0; the FID-0066 opt-out contract is unchanged).

variants.name: fire_rate_optout
variants.note: Pre-FID-0066 guard full-auto cadence on the CURRENT default world (FID-0014/0046/0117 ON, only fire-rate-authentic OFF). Single-source byte-identity anchor consumed by tools/fidelity/guard_fire_rate_symmetry_smoke.sh (was a stale duplicate literal there — AUDIT-0020) and re-verified by the generic tape gate. Re-record atomically with sim_state_hash on any default-world rebaseline: tape_regression.sh --record replays declared variants and prints their fresh hashes. Confirmed stable across 3+ isolated runs 2026-07-13; the cadence ratio (ON==3xOFF) remains an independent assertion in the guard lane, not substituted by this hash. Re-baselined 2026-07-15 (ea95db4614bcd952->5d8b8a0ecd40de24) atomically alongside the default sim_state_hash for FID-0131 (Dam pool-region build-sensitivity); regenerated by replaying this tape under GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0 on the current build. Re-baselined 2026-07-19 (5d8b8a0ecd40de24->aff5de8ca6522bc5) atomically alongside the default sim_state_hash for FID-0132 (collision-hull corner fix); regenerated by replaying this tape under GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0 on the fixed binary (stable across 3 runs, trace flags hash-neutral).

variants.name: widescreen_widen_optout
variants.note: FID-0143 sim-sandbox parity anchor: the widescreen widen (default ON) must leave this tape's hash IDENTICAL with the widen on or off — the widen's sim sandbox (state snapshot/restore, sim draw-list query snapshot, draw-only matrix band, faithful-order slot pre-warm) is proven leak-free on this route. A divergence here means a new render->sim leak in the widen pass.
